friction_items.description: 211 unique tool names are exposed by server.py (spec said 344 — even the lower count is overwhelming). A graph-theory conjecture researcher needs maybe 12-15 of them (sys_boot, sys_workspace_scaffold, tool_protocols_list, tool_route/quick_route, sys_protocol_get, sys_protocol_next, mem_log, tool_search, tool_redteam_review, tool_theory_math_lean_check, tool_theory_math_coq_check, tool_theory_math_dep_graph, tool_path_finalize). The remaining ~196 are empirical/figure/dashboard/slurm/data noise. A natural-language requester will get distracted listing the wrong tool when several names look plausible (tool_search vs tool_search_arxiv vs tool_search_semantic_scholar vs sys_semantic_tool_search — four near-duplicates for one intent).
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 9 consolidation: collapse audit_* family (~24 tools) into one tool_audit with a `kinds` array; collapse search_* family into tool_search with `source` param; hide adapter tools behind pack-detection so they don't surface unless the project has SLURM/Cytoscape/etc. Target ~80 tools.

friction_items.description: proof_verification_workflow step 1 (declare_step_contract) forces the researcher to write a step_summary.yaml with `step_intent: proof`, `figure_required: false`, `literature_required: true` BEFORE they can even draft the claim. This is bureaucratic overhead pulled from empirical workflows. A mathematician sketching a contradiction proof on a graph conjecture does not think in `step_summary.yaml` schemas — they think in claim + witness + counter-attempt. The contract step exists only because the audit framework assumes empirical defaults and the theory pack has to negate them defensively.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Make step_intent auto-detected from pack registration. If theory_math pack is active, default figure/table/empirical-literature gates to OFF without requiring the researcher to author a YAML. Move the contract block into protocol metadata, not into a researcher-authored step.

friction_items.description: tool_theory_math_lean_check returns status='warning' and writes a hint file when Lean is missing, but the envelope is still _ok() (success). A researcher who issues 'check my proof in Lean' will read 'success' at the top of the JSON and assume verification passed. The actual install hint is buried inside `data.hint_path`. This is exactly the silent-failure pattern that proof workflows are supposed to prevent.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Return _err() or a distinct status='tool_unavailable' envelope (not nested under 'success') when Lean/Coq are missing. The status field at the top level should be 'error' or 'unavailable', not 'success' with a warning sub-status.

friction_items.description: proof_verification_workflow step 1 sets `literature_required: true` with verdict IMPORTED_AS_CITED, but the literature_per_step protocol and tool_audit_step_literature are built around AGREES/DISAGREES/EXTENDS empirical verdicts. The audit blocks tool_path_finalize unless every claim has one of those verdicts. A theorem citing Erdős 1947 does not 'AGREE' or 'DISAGREE' with Erdős — it imports the result. Researchers will be forced to either fake an AGREES verdict, pass override_literature_gate=true (defeating the gate), or wrestle with an audit error message about missing Discussion blocks for a verdict that doesn't apply.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add IMPORTED_AS_CITED as a first-class verdict in tool_audit_step_literature's enum, and exempt it from the Discussion-implication requirement. Or: auto-skip the step-literature audit when the active pack is theory_math AND step_intent=proof.

friction_items.description: proof_verification_workflow.yaml step `formal_check` references `tool_theory_lean_check` and `tool_theory_coq_check`, but the actual registered tool names are `tool_theory_math_lean_check` and `tool_theory_math_coq_check`. A researcher (or LLM running the protocol) following the protocol literally will get 'tool not found' errors. This is a documentation/code drift bug.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Edit proof_verification_workflow.yaml step formal_check to use the correct tool names, OR register aliases `tool_theory_lean_check -> tool_theory_math_lean_check` in _ALIASES.

friction_items.description: A researcher saying 'search the literature for prior work on the Erdős distinct-distances conjecture' sees six plausible tools: tool_search, tool_search_arxiv, tool_search_crossref, tool_search_pubmed, tool_search_semantic_scholar, tool_search_web, plus sys_semantic_tool_search (which is for finding TOOLS not literature). For a math conjecture, only arxiv + semantic_scholar are appropriate (Crossref is OK, PubMed is biomedical, web is noisy). Nothing in the protocol or tool descriptions tells the researcher which one to use for theory work.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Consolidate into a single tool_search with `domain: arxiv|crossref|pubmed|semantic_scholar|web|auto` parameter; default to `auto` and route based on active pack (theory_math => arxiv+semantic_scholar).

friction_items.description: lean_integration.yaml mentions 'whnf failures', 'unification metavariables', 'typeclass synthesis loops', 'simp divergence', 'definitional unfolding' in the description block. A graph theorist who has never used Lean (the most common case for the trigger 'formalise this in Lean') will bounce off this. The protocol is correct but assumes Lean fluency to read it.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Open with a 2-sentence plain-English summary ('Translates your informal proof into Lean 4 code so the type checker can validate every step. Best for foundational results you want bulletproof.'). Move the error-pattern jargon into the debug_error_patterns step where the reader has already chosen to engage with it.

friction_items.description: The scenario is specifically a graph-theory conjecture, but the theory_math pack has zero graph-theory specific scaffolding — no protocol for 'characterise the extremal graph', 'pick a parameter to induct on', 'forbidden subgraph reduction', 'probabilistic method setup'. The 8 protocols are all domain-agnostic (proof workflow, lemma library, conjecture tracking, dep graph, Lean, Coq, strategy, paper structure). A graph theorist sees nothing that maps to their daily verbs.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either add a graph_theory subdomain (with 2-3 protocols: extremal_setup, probabilistic_method, forbidden_subgraph) or rename the pack 'formal_proofs' to set correct expectations. Don't ship 'theory_math' branding without combinatorics/graph-theory content.

friction_items.description: proof_strategy_selection.yaml has 7 steps to produce a single named strategy + a 3-sentence justification. Steps 3 (enumerate_candidate_strategies — 6 sentences), 5 (outline_default_scaffold — 3-5 lines), and 6 (flag_canonical_mistakes — preset list) are checklist-y and feel like assignment-grading rather than research support. A working mathematician picks a strategy in 30 seconds; the protocol asks for ~20 minutes of structured writing.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Collapse steps 3+4+5+6 into a single step 'commit to a strategy with a one-paragraph rationale and a 3-line scaffold'. Keep the 6-strategy menu in the editorial_voice as a reference rather than mandating per-strategy sentences.

friction_items.description: tool_theory_math_dep_graph regex-parses .lean/.v files for theorems and imports. The description says 'approximate (lexical, not type-checked) but accurate enough to spot which lemmas a result transitively depends on'. In practice, lexical extraction misses anonymous proofs, `by`-blocks that use lemmas via `exact`, and any lemma referenced via tactic search (`exact?`, `apply?`). A researcher who trusts the dep graph will under-count dependencies and ship a paper whose theorem-dependency-graph claim is wrong.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either invest in `lean --print-dependencies` integration (when Lean is present) or downgrade the description to 'spots top-level explicit imports; misses tactic-mediated dependencies'. The current framing oversells.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_step_literature description says 'Blockers are hard stops for tool_path_finalize unless override_literature_gate=true is passed.' For a researcher who hits a blocker, the error path is: read tool_path_finalize output, find a reference to the audit, run tool_audit_step_literature, parse its output, find the specific claim missing a Verdict, edit findings_vs_literature.md, re-run. Five hops to fix one missing line.
friction_items.suggested_fix: When tool_path_finalize is blocked by an audit, surface the specific blocker (file + line + missing field) in its own output, not as a pointer to another audit tool.

friction_items.description: research_os_theory_math/__init__.py declares __version__ = '1.9.3', but the core just released 1.11.0 (per task context) and is bumping to 2.0.0. Pack versions silently drift from the core version. A researcher inspecting `sys_packs_installed` sees mismatched versions and wonders if there's a compat issue.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either sync pack versions to core on each release (release-prep phase), or document that packs version independently. Don't let them drift silently.

convoluted_tools: tool_search vs tool_search_arxiv vs tool_search_crossref vs tool_search_pubmed vs tool_search_semantic_scholar vs tool_search_web vs sys_semantic_tool_search (7 near-duplicates)

tool_audit_step_completeness vs tool_audit_step_literature vs tool_audit_quality_full vs tool_audit_synthesis vs tool_audit_coherence vs tool_audit_findings_diff vs tool_audit_findings_query (audit family overload — 24 tool_audit_* tools total)

tool_plan vs tool_plan_advance vs tool_plan_clear vs tool_plan_next_step vs tool_plan_step vs tool_plan_step_grounded vs tool_plan_turn vs tool_synthesize_plan (8 plan-family tools, unclear which to invoke from a proof workflow)
tool_route vs tool_quick_route vs tool_semantic_route vs sys_semantic_tool_search (4 routing entry points)
tool_grounding_register vs tool_grounding_verify vs tool_ground vs tool_ground_from_context (4 grounding variants)
tool_lessons vs tool_lessons_consult vs tool_lessons_record vs tool_dead_end_lessons vs tool_mistake_replay (5 lessons-family)
tool_step_iterate vs tool_step_iterations_list vs tool_step_revision_options vs tool_step_complete vs tool_promote_to_step (5 step-lifecycle tools)
confusing_protocols: proof_verification_workflow — step 1 (declare_step_contract) is empirical-framework leakage; researcher must author a step_summary.yaml just to say 'this is a proof, not an experiment'

lean_integration — opens with Lean jargon (whnf, metavariables, typeclass synthesis, simp divergence) before any plain-English orientation; will alienate first-time Lean users who are the primary trigger audience

lemma_library — semver applied to math lemmas is a useful metaphor but the protocol mandates a 6-step workflow including audit-fan-out for every revision; for a working draft where lemmas change daily, this is heavyweight
theory_paper_structure — useful but 6 sections + editorial-voice rules + quality bars; for a 4-page conference paper it overshoots
recommendations: Consolidate audit_* family: replace 24 tool_audit_* tools with one tool_audit({kinds: [...], scope: ...}) that dispatches internally. Saves ~23 names from the surface.
Consolidate search_* family: collapse 6 search tools into tool_search({source, query, ...}) with pack-aware default routing.
Hide empirical-only tools (dashboard, figure, slurm, sensitivity, power, preregister) behind pack detection — do not advertise them when the active project's pack is theory_math.
Fix the tool-name drift in proof_verification_workflow.yaml: change `tool_theory_lean_check` to `tool_theory_math_lean_check` (same for coq).
Change tool_theory_math_lean_check / coq_check to return status='unavailable' (not nested 'success') when the binary is missing — so the top-level status is honest.
Add IMPORTED_AS_CITED as a first-class literature verdict, OR auto-waive tool_audit_step_literature for proof-intent steps in theory_math pack.
Move the step_summary.yaml contract authoring out of proof_verification_workflow.step 1 — auto-emit it from the pack's protocol metadata so researchers don't author bureaucracy.
Add a graph-theory subdomain (or 2-3 representative protocols) to the theory_math pack, OR rename the pack to set correct expectations.
Trim proof_strategy_selection from 7 steps to 3-4; the current shape feels like grading homework, not supporting a working theorist.
Sync research_os_theory_math.__version__ to the core release version during release-prep so packs don't silently drift from 1.9.3 while core moves to 2.0.0.
Rewrite tool_theory_math_dep_graph's description to honestly state 'top-level imports only; misses tactic-mediated dependencies'. Or invest in `lean --deps`.
Add a plain-English orientation paragraph to the top of lean_integration.yaml description before the whnf/metavariable jargon.
